flaky.reason: Passed on rerun without changes — toolkit init hit 429 on first run due to shared API rate limit exhaustion; recovered on second attempt

blocked.bug_description: The Bitbucket toolkit has two distinct 429 failure modes: (1) Toolkit initialization crash — BitbucketCloudApi.__init__ calls workspace.repositories.get() at construction time with no retry or backoff. A 429 response raises ToolException immediately, leaving the test with 'No toolkit tools provided'. (2) Mid-execution 429 — list_branches, get_files_list, _get_branch, and delete_branch all propagate 429 HTTPError directly as ToolException with no retry. The shared Bitbucket API token is rate-limited across concurrent test runs, causing the entire suite to fail.
blocked.expected_behavior: Toolkit initialization and all API calls should implement exponential backoff with at least 3 retries on 429 responses before raising ToolException
blocked.actual_behavior: Any 429 response immediately raises ToolException — at init time this leaves the toolkit with zero tools; during execution it fails the test step
blocked.error_location: cloud_api_wrapper.py:343 (__init__), cloud_api_wrapper.py:349 (list_branches), cloud_api_wrapper.py:452 (get_files_list), cloud_api_wrapper.py:408 (_get_branch via get_file), api_wrapper.py:286 (delete_branch)

commit_details.skip_reason: No test code fixes applied — all 23 failures are caused by Bitbucket API rate limiting (429). 2 tests are flaky (passed on rerun), 21 are blocked by SDK-level missing retry logic. No YAML changes were made.
